scenario: Atomic state (Jotai) vs slice state (Zustand) for fine-grained updates.
context: App with many independent state pieces.
alex_initial_position: Jotai atoms. Each piece is independent atom.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Atoms compose via derived atoms. Granular subscriptions free.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Zustand slices + selectors achieve granularity.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Derived atom dependencies are explicit and typed.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Devtools and ecosystem for Zustand are stronger.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For ultra-granular state: Jotai. For simpler: Zustand.

context_dependent_answer: Many independent pieces: Jotai. Centralized: Zustand.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors think state is one bucket. Senior insight: state has natural granularity. Tools match it.

scenario: Custom React.memo comparator vs structural sharing.
alex_initial_position: Custom comparator. Compare only relevant fields.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Comparator skips re-render when irrelevant prop changes.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Custom comparators get out of sync with component logic.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For verified hot paths, comparator wins.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Structural sharing in state lib (Immer, Zustand) means props are stable.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Stable props first; custom comparator last resort.

context_dependent_answer: Stable props via state lib. Custom comparator only when profiled needed.
decision_framework: Stable props default. Comparator for profiled hot paths.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors over-use memo. Senior insight: memoization is for measured perf, not defensive.

scenario: FormData vs JSON for form submission.
sam_initial_position: JSON for non-file forms; FormData when needed.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: FormData for files. Multipart automatic.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For text-only, JSON is cleaner.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. FormData for files, JSON for text.

context_dependent_answer: Files: FormData. Text: JSON.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors base64-encode files into JSON. Senior insight: FormData is for files.

scenario: Polling backoff: linear vs exponential.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Exponential avoids thundering herd.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Linear with jitter has same property at lower max delay.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Exponential more conservative on bad networks.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Exponential with cap + jitter.

context_dependent_answer: Exponential with jitter and cap (~30s).
what_juniors_miss: Juniors retry immediately. Senior insight: bad networks recover slowly; backoff respects that.

scenario: Scoped vs global CSS for keyframes.
context: Animation keyframes used in multiple components.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Reuse via global definitions.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Component-local clearer ownership.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Same animation in 5 places = global.

context_dependent_answer: Reusable: global. One-off: local.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors duplicate keyframes. Senior insight: keyframes are reusable values.

scenario: Animation on enter vs entrance + on-scroll-into-view.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: On-scroll defers animation cost to when seen.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Hero is above-the-fold; on-mount appropriate.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Above-the-fold: on-mount. Below: on-scroll.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Above-fold: on mount. Below-fold: IntersectionObserver.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors animate on mount everything. Senior insight: animate when visible, not before.

scenario: Test in CI vs test locally only.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: CI gate prevents broken main. Tests run on consistent env.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Locally first faster feedback. CI as final gate.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both. Pre-commit hook (local) + CI gate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Pre-commit local + CI gate.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors skip local tests. Senior insight: fast local checks prevent CI failures and waste.

scenario: useRef typing: HTMLElement vs specific subtype.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Specific gives .value, .select() without cast.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: More specific = less reusable.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For typed input, specific. For polymorphic, generic.

context_dependent_answer: Match ref type to actual element.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use HTMLElement. Senior insight: specific types give specific methods.

scenario: requestAnimationFrame vs setTimeout for animations.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: rAF auto-pauses on hidden tabs. setTimeout doesn't.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For non-visual work, setTimeout fine.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For animation, rAF is mandatory.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Animation: rAF. Non-visual: setTimeout/scheduler.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors setTimeout animations. Senior insight: rAF syncs with display refresh.

scenario: Type checking in CI: separate step vs part of build.
alex_initial_position: Separate. Parallel with build, fail-fast on type errors.
sam_initial_position: Part of build. Build IS the typecheck.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Separate tsc --noEmit parallel to bundler. Faster CI.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Bundlers (Vite) skip type checking for speed.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Right - so type checking must be separate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. tsc --noEmit in CI.

context_dependent_answer: tsc --noEmit in CI parallel to build.

code_examples.code:
// .github/workflows/ci.yml
jobs:
  typecheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: pnpm install
      - run: pnpm typecheck  # tsc --noEmit
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: pnpm install
      - run: pnpm build  # vite build, skips types
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: pnpm test

what_juniors_miss: Juniors think bundler typechecks. Senior insight: most bundlers skip TS for speed. Separate typecheck job is essential.

scenario: Component lib npm publishing: single package vs sub-packages.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Sub-packages = independent versioning.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Maintenance overhead. Most components co-evolve.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Single package with sub-paths is the modern pattern.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Single package + sub-paths via exports field.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors split into many packages. Senior insight: sub-paths achieve same without N packages.

scenario: Error reporting: rate-limit vs send all.
context: Errors flood Sentry from one bug.
alex_initial_position: Rate-limit. 1 per unique error per session.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Flood masks other errors and costs money.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Need to know severity (how many users).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Sentry tracks occurrence count. One report per error per session sufficient.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Rate-limit + Sentry counters.

context_dependent_answer: Rate-limit unique errors. Aggregation in Sentry.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't rate-limit. Senior insight: one bug × 1000 users = 1000 reports. Aggregate.

scenario: Critical request chain: preload vs prefetch.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Preload tells browser to fetch now, high priority.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Prefetch is low-priority for next navigation.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both have roles. Preload for current page LCP, prefetch for next.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Preload LCP image/critical CSS. Prefetch likely next page.

code_examples.code:
<head>
  {/* Preload LCP image - high priority, current page */}
  <link rel="preload" as="image" href="/hero.webp" fetchpriority="high" />
  
  {/* Preload critical font */}
  <link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/woff2" href="/inter.woff2" crossorigin />
  
  {/* Prefetch likely-next-page bundle - low priority */}
  <link rel="prefetch" href="/assets/checkout.js" />
</head>

what_juniors_miss: Juniors mix preload/prefetch. Senior insight: preload = current high-priority; prefetch = next low-priority.

scenario: Hover-only menus on mobile.
context: Desktop hover menu has no touch equivalent.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tap-to-open matches desktop hover.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Tap-to-open requires JS. Always-visible works without.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: JS is universal on mobile sites.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Tap-to-open with JS.

context_dependent_answer: Tap to open on touch. Hover on desktop.
decision_framework: Click always. Hover enhancement for desktop.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors hover-only. Senior insight: touch doesn't hover. Click is universal.

scenario: Frontend caching layer between API and components.
alex_initial_position: React Query is the caching layer. Don't add another.
sam_initial_position: Custom layer for cross-cutting (auth, error handling).

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React Query caches + retries + refetches.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Custom fetch wrapper for auth headers, error mapping.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: That's a fetcher fn, not a caching layer.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. Fetcher wrapper + React Query.

context_dependent_answer: Fetcher wrapper + React Query.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors inline auth/error in queries. Senior insight: cross-cutting concerns belong in a single fetcher wrapper.

scenario: Cross-tab state sync.
context: User edits in one tab; other tabs should reflect.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: BroadcastChannel is purpose-built. Cleaner API.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Storage events work for same-origin.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Storage events require localStorage round-trip.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. BroadcastChannel direct.

context_dependent_answer: BroadcastChannel. Storage events as fallback.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't cross-tab sync. Senior insight: multi-tab usage is common; sync makes UX consistent.

scenario: Imperative dialog API (showDialog()) vs declarative <Dialog open>.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: const ok = await confirm('Delete?'); is ergonomic.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Declarative composes with React state.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Imperative for one-off async; declarative for UI state.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Imperative for one-off confirms. Declarative for stateful dialogs.
decision_framework: Imperative for confirms. Declarative for stateful.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors declarative for everything. Senior insight: confirms are inherently sequential. Imperative API matches.

scenario: Form library + headless UI (combobox, listbox).
alex_initial_position: Integrate form library with headless UI primitives.
sam_initial_position: Wrap headless UI in controlled component.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: RHF + react-aria Combobox: integrate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Wrap once, reuse with form integration.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Wrapper handles RHF integration. Used elsewhere.

context_dependent_answer: Wrap headless primitives with RHF integration.
decision_framework: Wrap headless primitives with form integration.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't integrate. Senior insight: form library + accessibility primitive = wrapper layer.

scenario: Server-side data with client interactivity.
context: Page rendered server-side; needs hydration for interaction.
alex_initial_position: React Query with initialData from server.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: React Query + initialData = hydrate without refetch.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Server Components handle rendering and pass to client.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For client interactivity, React Query needed for refetch/mutate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: RSC for static + client component for interactive.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Pattern: RSC fetch + pass to client as initialData.

context_dependent_answer: RSC fetch + pass initialData to client component.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors fetch in client only. Senior insight: server fetch + client hydration is fastest.

scenario: Mobile-first vs desktop-first responsive.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Mobile-first scales up better. Default styles work without media queries.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Sidebar layouts naturally desktop-first.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Mobile-first still works - default to stacked, expand at md:.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Mobile-first universal.

context_dependent_answer: Mobile-first. Tailwind's default direction.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors design desktop, breaks on mobile. Senior insight: mobile-first works on all sizes.

scenario: Animation orchestration: parent-driven vs child-driven.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Parent variants coordinate via staggerChildren.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Per-item delay simpler.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Parent variants support exit animations too.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted for Framer Motion. Plain CSS: per-item delay.

context_dependent_answer: Framer: parent variants. CSS: per-item delay.
decision_framework: Framer variants. CSS delay for plain.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors no stagger. Senior insight: staggered entrance feels polished.

scenario: Test data: factory functions vs object literals.
alex_initial_position: Factory functions. Override fields per test.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Factory: makeUser({ name: 'Alice' }) - just override what matters.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Object literal explicit. Test reads as data.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Factory + literal at edge cases.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Factories for common. Literals for edge cases.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors copy-paste data. Senior insight: factories reduce noise, focus on test variation.

scenario: Index signatures vs Record<K, V>.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Record<string, User> is clear.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: {[key: string]: User} is equivalent.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For closed key set: Record<'a' | 'b', User>. Better.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Record default.

context_dependent_answer: Record default.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors index signatures. Senior insight: Record is more explicit, supports closed keys.

scenario: Bundle size budget: per-route vs total.
alex_initial_position: Per-route budget. Each route's initial js < 100KB.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Per-route gives clear PR feedback.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: User loads multiple routes. Total matters.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both. Per-route warn, total fail.

context_dependent_answer: Per-route + total budgets.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't budget. Senior insight: bundle bloat is silent without budgets.

scenario: Container images: distroless vs slim.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Distroless ~50MB vs slim ~150MB. Cold start matters.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Debugging without shell hard.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Distroless has debug variant. Use for staging.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Distroless prod.

context_dependent_answer: Distroless prod. Slim for dev/staging.

code_examples.code:
# Multi-stage build
FROM node:20-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json pnpm-lock.yaml ./
RUN corepack enable && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
COPY . .
RUN pnpm build

# Production image - distroless
FROM gcr.io/distroless/nodejs20
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/server.js ./
CMD ["server.js"]

what_juniors_miss: Juniors use full node images. Senior insight: minimal images reduce attack surface and cold-start time.
